text: There is definitely a concept that when someone is a true and genuine friend that there is a connection created,

text: that is something beyond the ordinary connections that we make in every day interaction with one another in the context of the world.

source: Friendships and Runanubandh with Robert Svoboda _ Living with Reality __watch
speaker: Ram Dass

source: Friendships and Runanubandh with Robert Svoboda _ Living with Reality __watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Today's episode, we are focusing on the concept of friendship.

source: Friendships and Runanubandh with Robert Svoboda _ Living with Reality __watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So Dr. Soboda talks about it from the Vedic perspective, from the Karmic perspective.

source: Friendships and Runanubandh with Robert Svoboda _ Living with Reality __watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So if you are a person who's been thinking a lot about your friendships and maybe after the pandemic, you're questioning who is my real friend here.

source: Friendships and Runanubandh with Robert Svoboda _ Living with Reality __watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And where do I want to be with my friendships?

source: Friendships and Runanubandh with Robert Svoboda _ Living with Reality __watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And to the point, this will really help you be thinking about those relationships and how you want to nourish them.

source: Friendships and Runanubandh with Robert Svoboda _ Living with Reality __watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So without further ado, here is Dr. Soboda on friendship.

source: Friendships and Runanubandh with Robert Svoboda _ Living with Reality __watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: In this episode, I'm going to be talking about friendship.

source: Friendships and Runanubandh with Robert Svoboda _ Living with Reality __watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And I'd like to begin with the Bible verse, one that I learned early on in my life.

source: Friendships and Runanubandh with Robert Svoboda _ Living with Reality __watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: The 13th verse of the 15th chapter of the gospel according to John.

source: Friendships and Runanubandh with Robert Svoboda _ Living with Reality __watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And it goes something like this.

source: Friendships and Runanubandh with Robert Svoboda _ Living with Reality __watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: No greater love has someone than that person will lay down their life for their friends.

source: Friendships and Runanubandh with Robert Svoboda _ Living with Reality __watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So that always struck me is a very strong expression of how important friendship can be and should be.

source: Friendships and Runanubandh with Robert Svoboda _ Living with Reality __watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And what a responsibility friendship actually is.

source: Friendships and Runanubandh with Robert Svoboda _ Living with Reality __watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: A good friend of course often becomes equivalent to a family member.

source: Friendships and Runanubandh with Robert Svoboda _ Living with Reality __watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: The the those members of your family who are not blood relations, but are related to you.

source: Friendships and Runanubandh with Robert Svoboda _ Living with Reality __watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And some other way, of course there is relation by marriage, but often people become family members simply by being sincere and genuine friends.

source: Friendships and Runanubandh with Robert Svoboda _ Living with Reality __watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And certainly in my life friendship has been extraordinarily important to me.

source: Friendships and Runanubandh with Robert Svoboda _ Living with Reality __watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Most saliently because of the fact that I have traveled so much and I have spent so much time going from one place to another.

source: Friendships and Runanubandh with Robert Svoboda _ Living with Reality __watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And I'm really relying on the good wishes of others and I have been extremely fortunate to have in many different countries met many different people who I can honestly say have become.

source: Friendships and Runanubandh with Robert Svoboda _ Living with Reality __watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: Good friends and many of them have become very much like family.

source: Friendships and Runanubandh with Robert Svoboda _ Living with Reality __watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So the there is definitely a concept that when someone is a true and genuine friend that there is a connection created that is something beyond the ordinary connections that we make and every everyday interaction with one another in the context of the world.

source: Friendships and Runanubandh with Robert Svoboda _ Living with Reality __watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And this is something that is strongly reflected in our Indo European languages, particularly in Indo Iranian.

source: Friendships and Runanubandh with Robert Svoboda _ Living with Reality __watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: The word for friend in Sanskrit is mithra that appears in ancient Iranian, avastas, mithra.

source: Friendships and Runanubandh with Robert Svoboda _ Living with Reality __watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And both that word, mithra and Sanskrit appears to emerge from the root MITH.

source: Friendships and Runanubandh with Robert Svoboda _ Living with Reality __watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And mith means literally to unite to pair up to couple.

source: Friendships and Runanubandh with Robert Svoboda _ Living with Reality __watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And from it we get the Sanskrit word mithuna.

source: Friendships and Runanubandh with Robert Svoboda _ Living with Reality __watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And mithuna means twins like Gemini, the twins astrological twins.

source: Friendships and Runanubandh with Robert Svoboda _ Living with Reality __watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And mithuna means a couple.

source: Friendships and Runanubandh with Robert Svoboda _ Living with Reality __watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And mithuna or my tuna also means copulation when two people come together and unite physically that is also a coupling.

source: Friendships and Runanubandh with Robert Svoboda _ Living with Reality __watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And that's also a very intimate kind of connection and that can also produce a very intimate kind of friendly relation.

source: Friendships and Runanubandh with Robert Svoboda _ Living with Reality __watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So there is implicit in this word.

source: Friendships and Runanubandh with Robert Svoboda _ Living with Reality __watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: There is definitely a sense of very close connection.

source: Friendships and Runanubandh with Robert Svoboda _ Living with Reality __watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: There is even in Buddhism, a Buddha named my Treyah and Vimal Anandah spoke of a Rishi named my Treyah.

source: Friendships and Runanubandh with Robert Svoboda _ Living with Reality __watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: And my Treyah means friendliness.

source: Friendships and Runanubandh with Robert Svoboda _ Living with Reality __watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: So someone who's who's entire being is basically a met focused on manifesting friendliness, manifesting that quality of being willing to sacrifice oneself for those who one regards as one friends that that Jesus spoke of in that Bible verse.

source: Friendships and Runanubandh with Robert Svoboda _ Living with Reality __watch
speaker: Ram Dass
text: In fact, Vimal Anandah used to say that it was that very Rishi my Treyah.
